title: chloraea alpina
intro: Chloraea alpina, also known as the alpine orchid, is a type of flowering plant native to the alpine regions of New Zealand. The plant contains compounds with medicinal properties, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. Chloraea alpina has been reported to have health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Herbal preparations of Chloraea alpina are typically made by infusing the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea or by drying the plant for use in capsules or tinctures.
